records.summary: ConfigurationStepMaterializer is the per-step configuration frontier for app tasks, option contributions, registration admissions, open seams, and produced product claims.
records.rationale: Configuration is where evaluated app setup, framework registries, plugin registrations, and world construction meet. Future external-app or authoring pressure should preserve the distinction between recognizing configuration sequences, materializing each step, admitting registrations, and composing the app world.
guidance: Start here when a configuration chain step needs app-task products, option contributions, registration admissions, or source-associated open seams.
Keep receiver/sequence recognition separate from per-step product materialization and app-world composition.
Use configuration README and workspace/plugin architecture pressure before collapsing project-specific configuration calls into framework bundle assumptions.

anchors.summary: Per-step configuration materializer.
Configuration semantics, ordering, and bundle-admission notes.

records.summary: ConfigurationOptionShapeIssueMaterializer owns source-backed diagnostics for statically impossible framework configuration option paths.
records.rationale: Framework configuration customize callbacks can look structurally valid in JavaScript while still assigning an option path that the framework API does not accept. Keep those diagnostics in configuration recognition, not in downstream feature materializers such as i18n catalogs or router route construction.
guidance: Start here when a recognized framework configuration contribution looks semantically invalid even though the assignment itself evaluates.
Keep product-specific consumers strict: i18n translation catalogs should read valid initOptions.resources, while top-level I18nConfiguration.resources remains a configuration option-shape diagnostic.
Extend the option schema from exact framework API grounding before adding a new allowed shortcut or diagnostic.
Use dedicated pressure fixtures for invalid-but-common configuration shapes so valid fixtures can keep proving downstream semantic behavior.

anchors.summary: Configuration-owned materializer for framework option-shape diagnostics.
Pressure fixture for invalid top-level I18nConfiguration.resources assignment.
Configuration issue ownership and option-shape notes.
I18n catalog guidance that keeps initOptions.resources distinct from invalid top-level resources.

records.summary: AureliaAppWorldProjectConstructionFrame is intentionally large as a named phase frame for app-world construction.
records.rationale: This shape replaced a monolithic construct-and-emit chain with named phase methods. Future refactors should preserve the phase-frame intent unless Atlas shows repeated or misplaced phase ownership.
guidance: Keep phase expansion in the frame instead of rebuilding a monolithic app-world construction method.
If the frame grows, split by durable phase boundaries and source/project construction responsibilities.
Use app pressure timings to decide which phase needs a better inquiry algebra or cache point.

anchors.summary: App-world construction phase frame.
Live class shape.

records.summary: ConfigurationKernelPublication owns the shared kernel-record envelope for configuration products, source evidence, open seams, and configuration claims.
records.rationale: Configuration used to hand-spell product identity/materialization/source triples across emitters and materializers. This publication primitive is the intentional consolidation point; future configuration products should reuse it unless their ownership semantics genuinely differ.
guidance: Start here when configuration needs ConfigurationIdentity, MaterializedProduct, MaterializationRecord, source/evidence/provenance triples, open seams, or configuration-owned claims.
Keep sequence, step, app-root, app-task, option, and Aurelia facade publication on this shared primitive before introducing a new record helper.

ConfigurationIssuePublisher wraps the shared kernel publishIssueProduct primitive for issue products; source scanners may prepend scanner-owned source-span records, but they should not hand-spell issue evidence/provenance/identity/product envelopes.
If provenance feels too field-level for framework-owned configuration facts, inspect this boundary before changing individual materializers.

anchors.summary: Configuration-owned kernel publication primitive.
Configuration issue product publisher over the shared kernel issue-publication primitive.
Configuration publication and source/evidence/provenance guidance.

records.summary: ConfigurationKernelEmitter is the source-order configuration sequence emitter that coordinates app-frame and per-step materialization.
records.rationale: The emitter owns the configuration sequence boundary and batch commit, but should remain thin over publication, app-frame admission, and per-step products. Its size is a watchpoint for sequence orchestration growing back into app-root or registration emulation.
guidance: Start here when sequence ordering, sequence products, app-frame coordination, or configuration batch emission changes.
Keep per-step product creation in ConfigurationStepMaterializer and record envelopes in ConfigurationKernelPublication.
If new app admission facts appear, check AureliaAppFrameMaterializer before adding logic directly to the emitter.

anchors.summary: Configuration sequence emitter.
Configuration sequence, app-frame, and step materialization split.

records.summary: BindingScope, BindingContext, and OverrideContext are framework-shaped semantic models for runtime Scope lookup and template-visible names.
records.rationale: Binding scope sits between controller activation, template locals, expression lookup, and TypeChecker projection. It is intentionally modeled as a runtime concept with auLink anchors rather than as a TypeScript lexical scope or answer-local completion helper.
guidance: Start here when expression lookup, `$parent`, `$this`, template locals, repeat locals, or branch-local scope overlays change.
Keep runtime binding scope distinct from compiler resource scope and template-controller flow scope.
Use Scope product details for inquiry instead of peeking into controller construction or compiler-world internals.
Direct authored Scope.create/fromParent/getContext nullish-argument failures are configuration-owned source/API issues; do not model them as rendered binding lookup fallbacks.
Scope API issue spans should carry the boot-admitted source-file handle through sourceSiteForNode/sourceSpanAddressForSite. Do not fall back to filename re-resolution for this scanner-owned path.
Scope API issue products should publish through ConfigurationIssuePublisher after the scanner-owned source span is created.

anchors.summary: Runtime Scope semantic model.
Runtime BindingContext semantic model.
Runtime IOverrideContext semantic model.
Source/API materializer for direct runtime Scope nullish-argument diagnostics.
Shared literal-level nullish recognition used by source API diagnostics.
Pressure fixture for direct Scope API AUR0203/AUR0204 configuration diagnostics.
Binding-scope responsibilities and watchpoints.

records.summary: AppWorldResourceVisibilityFrame projects DI resource slots, configured framework resources, app root, and local dependencies into compiler-world resource visibility.
records.rationale: Resource visibility is a configuration-to-DI-to-template join, not private composer glue. Future router, plugin, or view-factory visibility rules should land here as explicit visibility semantics before compiler-world materialization consumes them.
guidance: Start here when compiler-world resource scope depends on container resource slots, configured framework catalogs, app-root resources, or local dependencies.
Keep visibility projection separate from app-world orchestration and resource definition convergence.
If router or plugin context changes template resource visibility, add a named visibility rule here rather than broadening compiler-world materializers blindly.

anchors.summary: Public resource visibility composer.
Visibility projection work frame.
App-world resource visibility guidance.

records.summary: AppTask-time mutations of framework services must materialize as compiler-world and observer-locator service state.
records.rationale: Framework corpus form pressure exposed AppTask callbacks that call IAttrMapper.useTwoWay(...) and NodeObserverLocator.useConfig(...). These are not fixture quirks: Aurelia applications can customize framework services during configuration, so semantic-runtime needs a durable recognition and threading layer rather than built-in-only AttrMapper/ObserverLocator instances.
guidance: Start here when AppTask callbacks mutate IAttrMapper, NodeObserverLocator, or adjacent framework services.
Direct or evaluated AppTask registry values must keep their AppTask observation attached; do not let the synthetic registry object erase the lifecycle callback.
AppTask factory calls are registry value creation, not standalone app configuration effects; registration admission is the boundary that should materialize the AppTask product.
Keep service customizations framework-shaped and auLink-grounded; do not push one-off rules into compiler or binding materializers.

Only treat an AppTask callback parameter as a container when the AppTask key is IContainer. Direct service keys such as NodeObserverLocator and IAttrMapper bind the parameter to that service, while no-key tasks and arbitrary DI keys must not become container locals just because their callback body calls .get(...).
Configuration recognition should identify service calls, while observation-owned helpers such as nodeObserverNodeConfigsFromUseConfigCall decode NodeObserverLocator config/accessor entries.

Duplicate NodeObserverLocator config/global config mappings are configuration-owned diagnostics: publish ConfigurationIssue products with exact runtime-html AUR0653 authority and keep the rest of the valid service state available to compiler-world observer lookup.

Configuration customization owns duplicate NodeObserverLocator mappings (AUR0653) and the allowDirtyCheck service-state handoff. The sibling node_observer_strategy_not_found code (AUR0652) is spent by observation target-access diagnostics only when observer lookup models the exact dirty-check-disabled framework path; host-node controller codes remain outside this frontier.

NodeObserverLocator.useConfig(...) service state is spent only when template/runtime binding semantics ask for an observer. Plain to-view .bind host-node access may still close through getAccessor/ElementPropertyAccessor unless an accessor override exists; two-way/from-view paths reach the configured observer.
Attach ConfigurationIssue product details only after the issue kernel records are committed; sidecar details without materialized products are invalid.
When recognition needs more expression closure, improve StaticEvaluator or the expression reader before adding ad hoc callback parsing.

anchors.summary: Recognizes AppTask service mutation calls into explicit service state.
Pass-local draft with real lifetime for customized AttrMapper/NodeObserverLocator state and configuration issue publication.
Compiler-world AttrMapper service that consumes recognized customization state.
Observer service that consumes recognized node observer customization state.
Observation-owned decoder for statically evaluated NodeObserverLocator.useConfig call shapes.
Source-backed configuration issue product publication for framework-service diagnostics.
Exact framework package/enum/member bridge for configuration-owned framework error codes.
Focused contract for AppTask NodeObserverLocator service customization, duplicate AUR0653 issues, and observer-lookup consumption of valid custom config.
Bridge memory back to auLink mirror rows for AttrMapper.

records.summary: AureliaAppWorldComposer is the current app-world orchestration boundary from configuration recognition into DI, built-in catalogs, service customization, and compiler worlds.
records.rationale: The class is not a new semantic model; it composes existing materializers. Keeping it tracked prevents future sessions from adding a parallel world-construction route when app analysis needs more configuration, DI, compiler-world, or source issue products.
guidance: Start here when app-world construction needs another project-level phase, but keep product semantics in the owning materializer below it.
Do not rebuild DI, built-in syntax/resource/renderer catalogs, compiler-world formation, or framework-service customization outside this composer.

Source issue products currently append to DI world construction here; if more source-diagnostic lanes appear, decide whether they belong in the owning area first and only then thread their project result through the composer.
Treat app-world performance pressure as phase-boundary pressure; profile before collapsing or bypassing this orchestration.

anchors.summary: Configuration-to-app-world orchestration.
Project pass that invokes app-world composition in the broader openApp pipeline.
Product coupling around app-world composition.
